identity.title: Token Reduction Packet-First Orchestration Plan
identity.summary: Three-phase delivery plan to reduce AI token/credit usage by replacing broad discovery with compact orchestration, worker, reconciliation, delta, and release packets.

goals: Reduce Complete & Release startup/context tokens by replacing broad discovery with compact phase orchestration packets.
Reduce subagent worker prompt tokens by giving workers bounded execution packets with ownership, validation, and handoff requirements.
Reduce long-running orchestration refresh and handoff review tokens through deterministic reconciliation verdicts and delta refresh.
Reduce release closeout discovery tokens through release-state, artifact-preparation, and final-result packets.
Preserve release safety by keeping policyApproval gates, schema validation, evidence references, and fallback-to-full-refresh behavior.
nonGoals: Do not remove policyApproval gates or human publish approval requirements.
Do not hide raw evidence; packets should reference expandable evidence instead of embedding it.
Do not finalize execution tasks until the plan artifact has been reviewed and accepted.
Do not replace existing runbooks; make routine prompts reference them selectively.

userStories.iWant: one compact command that classifies the phase path
userStories.soThat: I can start Complete & Release without rereading broad task state and runbooks
I can implement safely without rediscovering Workflow Cannon operating rules
I can manage parallel work without repeatedly expanding logs, diffs, and handoffs

userStories.iWant: release closeout packets and deterministic artifact prep
userStories.soThat: publish safety improves while closeout tokens decrease

valueAssessment.impact: High — directly targets the largest repeated token sinks in Complete & Release orchestration, worker startup, handoff reconciliation, and release closeout.
valueAssessment.rationale: The plan builds on existing command/readiness/delivery evidence patterns and keeps raw evidence expandable by reference.

riskAssessment.description: Packets may omit context that an agent needs for safe action.
riskAssessment.mitigation: Include stop conditions, expansion refs, conservative needs_full_refresh/needs_user_decision verdicts, and schema tests.

riskAssessment.description: Delta refresh can become stale or inconsistent with canonical task state.
riskAssessment.mitigation: Use generation/cursor validation and fall back to full packet refresh on mismatch.

riskAssessment.description: Prompt shortening could accidentally remove policy or approval reminders.
riskAssessment.mitigation: Keep dashboard authorization semantics and policy reminders as required prompt fields with snapshot tests.

riskAssessment.description: Release artifact automation could edit ambiguous changelog/schema content incorrectly.
riskAssessment.mitigation: Require dry-run, exact file change reporting, policyApproval on apply, and safe failure on ambiguity.

technicalImpact.compatibilityNotes: Adds packet/verdict commands and prompt references without removing existing broad commands or runbooks.
technicalImpact.migrationImpact: No data migration expected in Phase 1; Team Assignment packet metadata may require additive schema/store changes.

architecture.overview: Introduce reference-first packet commands as compact projections over existing task, team assignment, delivery evidence, and release readiness state. Commands return bounded JSON packets with digests, verdicts, refs, and safe fallback recommendations; existing raw evidence remains available through referenced commands/files.

decisions.rationale: Reduces repeated prompt and context expansion while preserving auditability.
Agents should stop or refresh when packet classification is ambiguous rather than inventing release state.

decisions.decision: Plan remains staged across three releases
decisions.rationale: Ships highest savings first while keeping each release independently valuable and testable.
The planner artifact should be reviewed before creating execution rows.

decisions.decision: Refine Phase 1 before finalizing into Phase 130
decisions.rationale: Maintainer chose to tighten acceptance criteria and sizing before materializing execution tasks, reducing downstream task churn.

decisions.decision: Store packet digests both on Team Assignment metadata and in a packet registry
decisions.rationale: Assignment metadata provides direct audit linkage, while the registry preserves packet lookup and stale-context analysis.

decisions.decision: Use existing guidance tier labels for worker model recommendations
decisions.rationale: agent-execution-packet should emit tier_1, tier_2, and tier_3 aligned to current kit.agentGuidance.tier semantics with rationale, avoiding stale model-family names.

decisions.decision: Implement prepare-release-artifacts as deterministic script plus wk command
decisions.rationale: File-edit mechanics should live in a deterministic script while dry-run/apply orchestration and policy approval live in a wk command.

decisions.decision: Split oversized medium WBS rows into focused packet, script, command, and evidence-check rows before Phase 130 finalization.
decisions.rationale: Focused rows clear review sizing warnings and make task finalization produce executable delivery units without hiding complexity behind low confidence.

uiUxDirection.summary: Dashboard Complete & Release prompt and assignment launch flows should become packet-first, shorter, and reference packet/verdict commands instead of embedding full procedures.

criticalPaths: Worker assignment uses agent-execution-packet and stores packet digest.
Clean handoff reconciliation avoids raw diff/log expansion.
Delta refresh falls back safely on stale cursor.
Release closeout packets expose missing evidence/version/publish safety.
outOfScopeTesting: Exact token accounting automation beyond metric hooks
Replacing all existing runbooks or broad commands

implementationGuidance: Prefer new read-only packet commands over prompt-only changes for token savings.
Keep packet bodies bounded and include references to runbooks, schemas, commands, files, and evidence instead of embedded prose.
Add stable schemas/contracts and snapshot fixtures for each packet/verdict shape.
Use existing readiness, delivery evidence, release evidence, recommendation, and team assignment helpers where possible.
Preserve JSON policyApproval and human publish approval gates for mutating or dangerous actions.
whatNotToDo: Do not make packets so terse that agents lose safety boundaries.
Do not embed full runbooks, task histories, logs, or diffs in packet output by default.
Do not allow stale deltas to silently apply.
Do not create execution tasks directly from this draft without review/accept/finalize.
Do not make release artifact preparation apply changes without a dry-run and policy approval.
assumptions: Current Phase 130 is the first candidate phase for Phase 1 delivery planning.
Existing task-engine, team-execution, dashboard, and release evidence command patterns can be extended without replacing canonical task state.
Token savings can initially be assessed through prompt size, broad-read count, and packet output bounds before exact accounting is automated.
Phase 1 will be refined before any Phase 130 execution tasks are finalized from this PlanArtifact.
Packet persistence should support both assignment-level audit metadata and a packet registry for later lookup.
Worker model recommendations should use existing guidance tier labels: tier_1, tier_2, and tier_3, plus rationale.
prepare-release-artifacts should split deterministic file-edit logic into a script and expose policy-gated dry-run/apply through a wk command.

wbs.title: Phase release orchestration packet
goalMapping: Reduce Complete & Release startup/context tokens by replacing broad discovery with compact phase orchestration packets.
Preserve release safety by keeping policyApproval gates, schema validation, evidence references, and fallback-to-full-refresh behavior.
wbs.approach: Implement a bounded read-only task-engine command that classifies the current phase path and returns compact next-action packet references instead of broad task history/runbook content.
acceptanceCriteria: Command returns phase key, workspace current/next phase, integration branch, task counts, terminal/non-terminal counts, ready summaries, blocked summaries, path classification, next action, and packet/runbook refs.
Supports empty, completed-only, active-work, blocked-needs-decision, release-ready, and release-blocked paths.
Output is bounded and excludes full task histories and full runbook text.
Dashboard-launched Complete & Release agent can run one command and know the appropriate path.
testingVerification: Unit test fixtures cover empty, completed-only, active-work, blocked, and release-ready phases
Contract snapshot test verifies packet schema and bounded reference fields
Dashboard prompt smoke test fixture verifies the command can launch from Complete & Release when applicable
wbs.riskNotes: Classification must be conservative when task sync or evidence state is ambiguous.
wbs.doneMeans: Orchestrator can classify phase release path from one compact packet.

generatedTaskPayload.title: Add phase-release-orchestration-state packet
generatedTaskPayload.approach: Implement bounded phase classification packet command with conservative verdicts and refs-over-content.
acceptanceCriteria: One command classifies phase path
All six path verdicts covered by tests
Output is bounded and reference-first

wbs.title: Worker execution packet core
goalMapping: Reduce subagent worker prompt tokens by giving workers bounded execution packets with ownership, validation, and handoff requirements.
Preserve release safety by keeping policyApproval gates, schema validation, evidence references, and fallback-to-full-refresh behavior.
wbs.suggestedTaskTitle: Add agent-execution-packet core for bounded worker starts
wbs.approach: Implement task-scoped worker packet command with ownership, validation, handoff, and stop-condition fields.
acceptanceCriteria: Packet includes task id, phase key, assignment intent, title/summary, acceptance criteria, owned/read-only/forbidden paths, base branch, suggested worker branch, validation commands, handoff contract, and stop conditions.
Worker can start without reading the full task queue.
Packet uses references over embedded playbook text.
Forbidden and read-only path boundaries are explicit in every packet.
testingVerification: Unit tests verify path ownership and forbidden path projection
Contract test fixture verifies worker packet shape stays reference-first
Instruction snapshot test verifies packet refs replace broad queue-reading guidance
wbs.riskNotes: Overly narrow packets could hide necessary safety context; include explicit expansion refs.
wbs.doneMeans: A worker packet provides bounded task context, ownership boundaries, validation commands, and explicit handoff refs for safe execution.

wbs.title: Team Assignment packet metadata
goalMapping: Reduce subagent worker prompt tokens by giving workers bounded execution packets with ownership, validation, and handoff requirements.
Preserve release safety by keeping policyApproval gates, schema validation, evidence references, and fallback-to-full-refresh behavior.
wbs.suggestedTaskTitle: Persist packet metadata on Team Assignments
wbs.approach: Extend Team Assignment creation/storage to reference the packet used for worker assignment, including digest, model tier, path bounds, handoff contract, and validation commands.
acceptanceCriteria: Assignment records can store packetId or packetDigest, modelTier, ownedPaths, forbiddenPaths, handoffContractId, and validationCommands.
Orchestrator can audit which context a worker received.
Stale packet context can be detected from assignment metadata.
Packet registry stores or resolves packet bodies/digests for later lookup alongside assignment metadata.

Assignment creation tests
Dashboard projection tests if surfaced
Integration test covers assignment metadata plus packet registry lookup.
wbs.doneMeans: Assignments durably reference packet metadata and operators can look up the registered packet body/digest used for worker context.

goalMapping: Reduce Complete & Release startup/context tokens by replacing broad discovery with compact phase orchestration packets.

wbs.suggestedTaskTitle: Shorten Complete & Release prompt around packet-first flow
wbs.approach: Update dashboard prompt generation to direct agents to phase-release-orchestration-state first and agent-execution-packet for worker assignment, while removing command-by-command runbook restatement.
acceptanceCriteria: Prompt is shorter than current Complete & Release prompt.
Prompt starts with phase-release-orchestration-state.
Prompt directs worker starts through agent-execution-packet.
Prompt preserves dashboard authorization semantics and policy reminders.
Prompt instructs maintainers to refine/review the Phase 1 plan before finalizing execution tasks when review warnings remain.

No full runbook restatement in prompt
Dashboard command smoke test
Prompt snapshot verifies refine-before-finalize guidance is present when the plan has warnings.
wbs.doneMeans: Dashboard Complete & Release starts with compact packet commands and a shorter prompt.

wbs.title: Assignment reconciliation preflight
goalMapping: Reduce long-running orchestration refresh and handoff review tokens through deterministic reconciliation verdicts and delta refresh.
Preserve release safety by keeping policyApproval gates, schema validation, evidence references, and fallback-to-full-refresh behavior.
wbs.approach: Implement a deterministic handoff/reconciliation preflight that checks structured handoff evidence, path ownership, validation records, PR target, and policy risks before the orchestrator reads raw logs or diffs.
acceptanceCriteria: Verdicts include ready_to_reconcile, needs_worker_followup, needs_orchestrator_review, needs_user_decision, and unsafe.
Clean handoff returns ready_to_reconcile with compact evidence refs.
Missing validation or PR evidence returns specific remediation.
Forbidden path changes return unsafe or needs-review verdict.
testingVerification: Unit test fixture covers clean handoff verdict
Unit test fixture covers missing validation or PR evidence remediation
Integration test fixture covers forbidden path changes
Contract test fixture verifies PR target evidence refs
wbs.riskNotes: Needs clear separation between advisory warnings and hard unsafe verdicts.
wbs.doneMeans: Orchestrator can reconcile clean worker handoffs from a compact verdict packet.

generatedTaskPayload.title: Add assignment-reconciliation-preflight verdict command
generatedTaskPayload.approach: Implement structured assignment handoff preflight with compact verdicts and evidence refs.
acceptanceCriteria: All verdicts are represented
Clean handoff can reconcile without raw diff expansion
Unsafe path changes are detected

wbs.title: JSON-first worker handoff enforcement
goalMapping: Reduce long-running orchestration refresh and handoff review tokens through deterministic reconciliation verdicts and delta refresh.
Preserve release safety by keeping policyApproval gates, schema validation, evidence references, and fallback-to-full-refresh behavior.
wbs.suggestedTaskTitle: Make worker handoffs JSON-first and machine-checkable
wbs.approach: Define and enforce a structured handoff schema for task id, assignment id, summary, changed files, commands run, acceptance criteria status, PR/ref, risks, blockers, and follow-ups, with prose as secondary.
acceptanceCriteria: Structured handoff fields are validated.
Assignment reconciliation preflight consumes handoff data as source of truth.
Prose summary remains optional/secondary.
Missing required handoff fields produce actionable findings.
testingVerification: Schema test fixtures validate required and missing worker handoff fields
Integration test verifies preflight consumes JSON handoff as the source of truth
Instruction snapshot test verifies prose remains secondary to structured handoff data
wbs.doneMeans: Worker handoffs validate as structured JSON, drive reconciliation preflight, and preserve prose as secondary context.

generatedTaskPayload.title: Make worker handoffs JSON-first and machine-checkable
generatedTaskPayload.approach: Add structured handoff schema/enforcement and update worker instructions to make JSON fields authoritative.
acceptanceCriteria: Required handoff fields validate
Preflight uses JSON handoff

wbs.title: Phase drain delta refresh
goalMapping: Reduce long-running orchestration refresh and handoff review tokens through deterministic reconciliation verdicts and delta refresh.
wbs.approach: Implement a generation/cursor-aware delta command that returns changed tasks, newly ready work, completed assignments, active assignments, blocked tasks, updated phase path, next action, and new cursor after the initial packet.
acceptanceCriteria: Command returns only material changes since a valid generation/cursor.
Stale or invalid cursor returns safe full-refresh recommendation.
Newly ready work and blocked decisions are easy to assign/review from delta output.
Delta output caps changed task and assignment arrays to configured limits and returns overflow refs when limits are exceeded.
testingVerification: Unit test fixture covers delta output after task transition
Integration test fixture covers delta output after assignment completion
Unit test fixture covers stale cursor full-refresh recommendation
Contract test assertion verifies bounded output and overflow refs
wbs.doneMeans: Long-running orchestrators refresh from deltas instead of broad state reads.

wbs.title: Deterministic release artifact preparation script
goalMapping: Reduce release closeout discovery tokens through release-state, artifact-preparation, and final-result packets.
Preserve release safety by keeping policyApproval gates, schema validation, evidence references, and fallback-to-full-refresh behavior.
wbs.suggestedTaskTitle: Add deterministic release artifact preparation script
wbs.approach: Implement deterministic file-edit script mechanics for release artifact preparation with dry-run diff generation and ambiguity checks.
acceptanceCriteria: Dry-run reports exact files and changes the script would make.
Script preserves existing manual changelog context and fails safely on ambiguous edits.
Routine release prep does not require reading full changelog or schema content.
Script can run independently of wk command policy handling.
testingVerification: Unit test verifies dry-run diff generation independent of wk command policy handling
Unit test fixture covers ambiguous changelog failure
Integration test fixture applies script changes in a temp workspace
wbs.doneMeans: Release artifact file edits are deterministic, dry-runnable, and safe on ambiguous changelog or version state.

goalMapping: Reduce release closeout discovery tokens through release-state, artifact-preparation, and final-result packets.
